preamble: The transformation of the open central vowel [a] into the back rounded diphthong [ou] involves both rounding and backing, often triggered by surrounding labial or velar environments.

examples.note: Development of Middle English long [a:] into earlier [o:] stages before further raising.
Rounding of [a] in the context of [w] in certain varieties.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
